chosen_logo.why: JSON-LD knowledge_graph_logo.png from Apple itself — the canonical PNG Apple wants search engines and consumers to use for their brand identity. Same-origin (apple.com), so the embed_logo cross-origin fix path works.

brand_primary.name: --color-brand-onyx
brand_primary.evidence: Apple's signature pure black — used as cover/hero background on dark-mode pages (privacy, macbook-pro). Computed body bg on macbook-pro and privacy is rgb(0,0,0); literal #000000 appears 126× across all six pages.

brand_secondary.name: --color-brand-blue
brand_secondary.evidence: Apple's iconic accent blue — the only saturated colour Apple uses, reserved for CTAs ('Shop', 'Compare all models', inline links). 40× in CSS literals across all six pages.

ink.name: --color-brand-ink
ink.evidence: Apple Ink — slightly warmer than pure black. Computed body color rgb(29,29,31) on iphone/environment/accessibility/newsroom. 36× in CSS. Apple uses this instead of pure black for body text on light surfaces.

paper.name: --color-brand-paper
paper.evidence: Pure white — primary surface for product and content pages (iphone, environment, accessibility). The light half of Apple's two-mode design.

accents.name: --color-brand-mist
accents.evidence: Apple's signature off-white surface — used for newsroom body, page section dividers, secondary cards. 16× in CSS.

accents.name: --color-brand-mute
accents.evidence: Muted secondary text grey — used for h2/eyebrow type and disclaimers. Computed h2 colour on macbook-pro and accessibility.

accents.name: --color-brand-violet
accents.evidence: Apple Intelligence violet accent — used sparingly on AI-feature highlights. 6× in CSS; reserve for one accent moment per deck.

neutrals.name: --color-brand-graphite
neutrals.evidence: Slightly lighter than pure black — used as panel backgrounds on dark pages. 6× in CSS.

neutrals.name: --color-brand-rule
neutrals.evidence: Apple's standard divider grey — derived from Apple's published HIG.

display_font.evidence: Computed h1 font on iphone and privacy: 'SF Pro Display, SF Pro Icons, Helvetica Neue'. iPhone h1 sits at 80 px — Apple's billboard-scale headline treatment. SF Pro Display is Apple's proprietary system display face.

body_font.evidence: Computed body font on every page: 'SF Pro Text, SF Pro Icons, Helvetica Neue, sans-serif'. 67× in font frequencies. The system pairs SF Pro Display for large headlines with SF Pro Text for body — both shipped via Apple's web font CDN.

spacing_radius_shadow.shadow_style: flat — only subtle elevation on hover product cards
spacing_radius_shadow.evidence: Computed h1, button, and a have radius:0px on every page; the only exception is newsroom 'a' tags at radius:8px (Apple's current pill-button style). No box-shadow on top-level chrome. Apple commits to sharp rectangles + generous whitespace + restrained density.

aesthetic.voice_register: executive precision; product copy speaks at the level of one sentence per idea — 'iPhone.', 'Explore the lineup.', 'Privacy. That's iPhone.' The brand voice is short declaratives followed by white space.
